name: safe_agentic_workflow
description: Autonomous coding pipeline. Run for any request to write or change code: derives requirements, understands the codebase, reads docs, plans, GENERATES a per-task coding workflow, runs it, validates with the project's tests, and loops through an acceptance gate until the solution passes or the max loop count is reached, then documents the result.

variables.description: The feature/change the user asked for, as originally stated.
The specific, testable definition of done derived from the task.
What the relevant existing code does: files, modules, patterns, and conventions the change must fit.
Relevant facts from project docs (AGENTS.md, README, design notes): how to build/test, constraints, and conventions.
The implementation plan: the relevant areas to change and the ordered coding steps, one entry per area.
A short snake_case identifier derived from the task, used as the generated coding workflow's name/filename (e.g. add_dark_mode).
The name of the generated per-task coding workflow: task_slug followed by _workflow.
How many validation/gate cycles have run so far.
The maximum number of validation/gate cycles allowed before the pipeline stops looping (default 3).
Whether the project's test command exited successfully on the latest implementation.
The captured stdout/stderr and exit status of the latest test run.
Whether the acceptance gate judged the implementation complete and correct against the acceptance criteria.
When the gate rejects: the specific defects/gaps the next coding pass must fix. Empty when accepted.

settings.action: Requirements analyst. Read task_description and derive acceptance_criteria: a specific, testable definition of done for this coding task. If the task already implies clear criteria, formalize them; do not invent scope beyond what was asked. Also derive task_slug: a short snake_case identifier for the task (e.g. 'add a dark-mode toggle' -> add_dark_mode), and set coding_workflow_name to task_slug + '_workflow'. Set max_loops to 3 and gate_iteration to 0.

Explore the codebase for the areas this task touches. Use read/search/list tools (never guess). Record in codebase_notes: the relevant files and modules, the existing patterns and conventions the change must follow, and the integration points the new code will connect to.

Read the project's docs relevant to this task (AGENTS.md, README, and any design/architecture notes). Record in doc_notes: how to build and TEST the project (the exact test command), and any constraints or conventions the implementation must respect.

Using acceptance_criteria, codebase_notes, and doc_notes, write plan: the implementation plan for this task. Break the work into the relevant AREAS to change (as many as the task needs) and, for each area, the ordered coding step(s) that implement it. Keep it concrete and grounded in the actual files found. This plan drives the generated coding workflow's steps.

Generate the per-task coding workflow from plan and write it under the coding/ subdir. Follow the 'botcircuits-coding-workflow-authoring' skill contract: the generated workflow must START with a check_revision branch that does a targeted fix pass when revision_findings is non-empty, then have one coding step per area from plan (dynamic count), then a final self-check step; it must contain NO question steps and NO review/approval steps (those live here in the pipeline). Write the intent-only source to a file and run: `botcircuits workflow generate --from <that file> --name <coding_workflow_name> --subdir coding --build`. Confirm the build succeeded (the runnable artifact exists at .botcircuits/workflows/.build/coding/<coding_workflow_name>.json).

Execute the generated coding workflow with shell_exec: `botcircuits workflow run --name <coding_workflow_name> --initial-args '{"revision_findings": "<current revision_findings, empty on the first pass>"}'`. This performs the actual code changes. Report what it did.

Run the project's test command (from doc_notes) with shell_exec and capture the full output and exit status into validation_output. Set tests_passed to true only if the command exited 0. Do not narrate success without a real passing run — the pass/fail is the exit code.

Acceptance gate. Increment gate_iteration by 1. Judge the implementation against acceptance_criteria using tests_passed and validation_output. If the tests passed AND the criteria are fully and correctly met, set solution_accepted=true and clear revision_findings (empty). Otherwise set solution_accepted=false and write revision_findings: the specific defects, failing tests, and unmet criteria the next coding pass must fix.

Write a concise implementation summary: what was built/changed (grounded in the actual code produced), which acceptance_criteria were met and any that were not, the final test result from validation_output, and any known limitations or follow-ups. If the loop hit max_loops without acceptance, say so plainly and list the outstanding revision_findings.
